# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Bash script for deploying to AWS using the AWS CLI. The script should authenticate with AWS and perform deployment operations. Ensure the script can be used safely in both local development and CI/CD environments.

Load secrets from environment variables or a secret manager rather than embedding them in code.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -euo pipefail

# =============================================================================
# AWS Deployment Script
# Supports both local development and CI/CD environments
# =============================================================================

# ---- Configuration (override via environment variables) ----
AWS_REGION="${AWS_REGION:-us-east-1}"
AWS_PROFILE="${AWS_PROFILE:-}"
DEPLOYMENT_ENV="${DEPLOYMENT_ENV:-staging}"
APP_NAME="${APP_NAME:-myapp}"
S3_BUCKET="${S3_BUCKET:-}"
ECS_CLUSTER="${ECS_CLUSTER:-}"
ECS_SERVICE="${ECS_SERVICE:-}"
ECR_REPO="${ECR_REPO:-}"
DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG="${DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG:-latest}"
LAMBDA_FUNCTION_NAME="${LAMBDA_FUNCTION_NAME:-}"
LAMBDA_ZIP_FILE="${LAMBDA_ZIP_FILE:-}"
DEPLOYMENT_TYPE="${DEPLOYMENT_TYPE:-ecs}"  # ecs, s3, lambda
SECRET_MANAGER="${SECRET_MANAGER:-env}"   # env, aws-secrets-manager, aws-ssm

# ---- Logging ----
LOG_FILE="/tmp/${APP_NAME}-deploy-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log"

log() {
    local level="$1"
    shift
    local message="$*"
    local timestamp
    timestamp=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
    echo "[$timestamp] [$level] $message" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
}

log_info()  { log "INFO" "$@"; }
log_warn()  { log "WARN" "$@"; }
log_error() { log "ERROR" "$@"; }

# ---- Cleanup trap ----
cleanup() {
    local exit_code=$?
    if [[ $exit_code -ne 0 ]]; then
        log_error "Deployment failed with exit code $exit_code"
        log_error "Check log file: $LOG_FILE"
    fi
    # Remove any temporary credential files
    if [[ -n "${TEMP_CRED_FILE:-}" && -f "${TEMP_CRED_FILE:-}" ]]; then
        rm -f "$TEMP_CRED_FILE"
        log_info "Cleaned up temporary credential file"
    fi
    exit $exit_code
}

trap cleanup EXIT
trap 'log_error "Script interrupted"; exit 130' INT TERM

# ---- Secret Loading Functions ----

load_secrets_from_env() {
    log_info "Loading secrets from environment variables"

    if [[ -z "${AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID:-}" || -z "${AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY:-}" ]]; then
        if [[ -n "$AWS_PROFILE" ]]; then
            log_info "No access keys in env; using AWS profile: $AWS_PROFILE"
            return 0
        fi

        # Check if running with IAM role (EC2/ECS/Lambda)
        if aws sts get-caller-identity --region "$AWS_REGION" &>/dev/null; then
            log_info "Using IAM role credentials (instance profile or task role)"
            return 0
        fi

        log_error "AWS credentials not found. Set AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID/AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, AWS_PROFILE, or attach an IAM role."
        return 1
    fi

    export AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID
    export AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY
    if [[ -n "${AWS_SESSION_TOKEN:-}" ]]; then
        export AWS_SESSION_TOKEN
    fi

    log_info "AWS credentials loaded from environment variables"
    return 0
}

load_secrets_from_aws_secrets_manager() {
    local secret_name="${AWS_DEPLOY_SECRET_NAME:-${APP_NAME}/${DEPLOYMENT_ENV}/deploy-credentials}"
    log_info "Loading secrets from AWS Secrets Manager: $secret_name"

    local secret_value
    secret_value=$(aws secretsmanager get-secret-value \
        --secret-id "$secret_name" \
        --region "$AWS_REGION" \
        --query 'SecretString' \
        --output text 2>/dev/null) || {
        log_error "Failed to retrieve secret: $secret_name"
        return 1
    }

    # Parse JSON secret (expects keys: AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY)
    if command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
        export AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID
        AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=$(echo "$secret_value" | jq -r '.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID // empty')
        export AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY
        AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=$(echo "$secret_value" | jq -r '.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY // empty')
        local session_token
        session_token=$(echo "$secret_value" | jq -r '.AWS_SESSION_TOKEN // empty')
        if [[ -n "$session_token" ]]; then
            export AWS_SESSION_TOKEN="$session_token"
        fi
    else
        log_error "jq is required to parse Secrets Manager response"
        return 1
    fi

    # Clear the secret from the variable
    unset secret_value

    log_info "Secrets loaded from AWS Secrets Manager"
    return 0
}

load_secrets_from_ssm() {
    local prefix="${AWS_SSM_PREFIX:-/${APP_NAME}/${DEPLOYMENT_ENV}}"
    log_info "Loading secrets from AWS SSM Parameter Store: $prefix"

    export AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID
    AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=$(aws ssm get-parameter \
        --name "${prefix}/AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID" \
        --with-decryption \
        --region "$AWS_REGION" \
        --query 'Parameter.Value' \
        --output text 2>/dev/null) || {
        log_error "Failed to retrieve SSM parameter: ${prefix}/AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID"
        return 1
    }

    export AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY
    AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=$(aws ssm get-parameter \
        --name "${prefix}/AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY" \
        --with-decryption \
        --region "$AWS_REGION" \
        --query 'Parameter.Value' \
        --output text 2>/dev/null) || {
        log_error "Failed to retrieve SSM parameter: ${prefix}/AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY"
        return 1
    }

    log_info "Secrets loaded from SSM Parameter Store"
    return 0
}

# ---- Prerequisite Checks ----

check_prerequisites() {
    log_info "Checking prerequisites..."

    local missing=()

    if ! command -v aws &>/dev/null; then
        missing+=("aws-cli")
    fi

    if [[ "$DEPLOYMENT_TYPE" == "ecs" ]]; then
        if ! command -v docker &>/dev/null; then
            missing+=("docker")
        fi
    fi

    if ! command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
        missing+=("jq")
    fi

    if [[ ${#missing[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
        log_error "Missing required tools: ${missing[*]}"
        return 1
    fi

    log_info "All prerequisites met"
    return 0
}

# ---- Authentication ----

authenticate() {
    log_info "Authenticating with AWS (secret manager: $SECRET_MANAGER)..."

    case "$SECRET_MANAGER" in
        env)
            load_secrets_from_env || return 1
            ;;
        aws-secrets-manager)
            load_secrets_from_aws_secrets_manager || return 1
            ;;
        aws-ssm)
            load_secrets_from_ssm || return 1
            ;;
        *)
            log_error "Unknown secret manager: $SECRET_MANAGER"
            return 1
            ;;
    esac

    # Build AWS CLI args
    AWS_CLI_ARGS=("--region" "$AWS_REGION")
    if [[ -n "$AWS_PROFILE" ]]; then
        AWS_CLI_ARGS+=("--profile" "$AWS_PROFILE")
    fi

    # Verify authentication
    local caller_identity
    caller_identity=$(aws sts get-caller-identity "${AWS_CLI_ARGS[@]}" --output json 2>&1) || {
        log_error "AWS authentication failed: $caller_identity"
        return 1
    }

    local account_id arn
    account_id=$(echo "$caller_identity" | jq -r '.Account')
    arn=$(echo "$caller_identity" | jq -r '.Arn')
    log_info "Authenticated as: $arn (Account: $account_id)"

    return 0
}

# ---- Deployment Functions ----

deploy_to_ecs() {
    log_info "Starting ECS deployment..."

    if [[ -z "$ECS_CLUSTER" || -z "$ECS_SERVICE" || -z "$ECR_REPO" ]]; then
        log_error "ECS_CLUSTER, ECS_SERVICE, and ECR_REPO must be set for ECS deployment"
        return 1
    fi

    # Get ECR login
    local ecr_registry
    ecr_registry=$(aws ecr describe-repositories \
        "${AWS_CLI_ARGS[@]}" \
        --repository-names "$ECR_REPO" \
        --query 'repositories[0].repositoryUri' \
        --output text 2>/dev/null) || {
        log_error "Failed to describe ECR repository: $ECR_REPO"
        return 1
    }

    # Extract registry URL (without repo name)
    local registry_url
    registry_url=$(echo "$ecr_registry" | sed 's|/[^/]*$||')

    log_info "Logging into ECR: $registry_url"
    aws ecr get-login-password "${AWS_CLI_ARGS[@]}" | \
        docker login --username AWS --password-stdin "$registry_url" 2>&1 | \
        tee -a "$LOG_FILE" || {
        log_error "ECR login failed"
        return 1
    }

    local full_image="${ecr_registry}:${DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG}"

    # Build and push Docker image
    log_info "Building Docker image: $full_image"
    docker build -t "$full_image" . 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE" || {
        log_error "Docker build failed"
        return 1
    }

    log_info "Pushing Docker image: $full_image"
    docker push "$full_image" 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE" || {
        log_error "Docker push failed"
        return 1
    }

    # Get current task definition
    log_info "Retrieving current task definition for service: $ECS_SERVICE"
    local task_def_arn
    task_def_arn=$(aws ecs describe-services \
        "${AWS_CLI_ARGS[@]}" \
        --cluster "$ECS_CLUSTER" \
        --services "$ECS_SERVICE" \
        --query 'services[0].taskDefinition' \
        --output text) || {
        log_error "Failed to describe ECS service"
        return 1
    }

    # Get task definition and update image
    local task_def
    task_def=$(aws ecs describe-task-definition \
        "${AWS_CLI_ARGS[@]}" \
        --task-definition "$task_def_arn" \
        --query 'taskDefinition' \
        --output json) || {
        log_error "Failed to describe task definition"
        return 1
    }

    # Create new task definition with updated image
    local new_task_def
    new_task_def=$(echo "$task_def" | jq \
        --arg IMAGE "$full_image" \
        '.containerDefinitions[0].image = $IMAGE |
         del(.taskDefinitionArn, .revision, .status, .requiresAttributes, .compatibilities, .registeredAt, .registeredBy)')

    log_info "Registering new task definition..."
    local new_task_def_arn
    new_task_def_arn=$(aws ecs register-task-definition \
        "${AWS_CLI_ARGS[@]}" \
        --cli-input-json "$new_task_def" \
        --query 'taskDefinition.taskDefinitionArn' \
        --output text) || {
        log_error "Failed to register new task definition"
        return 1
    }

    log_info "New task definition: $new_task_def_arn"

    # Update service
    log_info "Updating ECS service: $ECS_SERVICE"
    aws ecs update-service \
        "${AWS_CLI_ARGS[@]}" \
        --cluster "$ECS_CLUSTER" \
        --service "$ECS_SERVICE" \
        --task-definition "$new_task_def_arn" \
        --force-new-deployment \
        --output json 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE" || {
        log_error "Failed to update ECS service"
        return 1
    }

    # Wait for deployment to stabilize
    log_info "Waiting for ECS service to stabilize (timeout: 10 minutes)..."
    aws ecs wait services-stable \
        "${AWS_CLI_ARGS[@]}" \
        --cluster "$ECS_CLUSTER" \
        --services "$ECS_SERVICE" || {
        log_error "ECS service did not stabilize within timeout"
        return 1
    }

    log_info "ECS deployment completed successfully"
    return 0
}

deploy_to_s3() {
    log_info "Starting S3 deployment..."

    if [[ -z "$S3_BUCKET" ]]; then
        log_error "S3_BUCKET must be set for S3 deployment"
        return 1
    fi

    local source_dir="${S3_SOURCE_DIR:-./build}"
    local s3_prefix="${S3_PREFIX:-}"

    if [[ ! -d "$source_dir" ]]; then
        log_error "Source directory does not exist: $source_dir"
        return 1
    fi

    local s3_target="s3://${S3_BUCKET}"
    if [[ -n "$s3_prefix" ]]; then
        s3_target="${s3_target}/${s3_prefix}"
    fi

    log_info "Syncing $source_dir to $s3_target"
    aws s3 sync "$source_dir" "$s3_target" \
        "${AWS_CLI_ARGS[@]}" \
        --delete \
        --no-progress 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE" || {
        log_error "S3 sync failed"
        return 1
    }

    # Invalidate CloudFront distribution if configured
    local cf_distribution_id="${CLOUDFRONT_DISTRIBUTION_ID:-}"
    if [[ -n "$cf_distribution_id" ]]; then
        log_info "Creating CloudFront invalidation for distribution: $cf_distribution_id"
        local invalidation_id
        invalidation_id=$(aws cloudfront create-invalidation \
            "${AWS_CLI_ARGS[@]}" \
            --distribution-id "$cf_distribution_id" \
            --paths "/*" \
            --query 'Invalidation.Id' \
            --output text) || {
            log_warn "CloudFront invalidation failed (non-fatal)"
        }

        if [[ -n "${invalidation_id:-}" ]]; then
            log_info "CloudFront invalidation created: $invalidation_id"

            if [[ "${WAIT_FOR_INVALIDATION:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
                log_info "Waiting for CloudFront invalidation to complete..."
                aws cloudfront wait invalidation-completed \
                    "${AWS_CLI_ARGS[@]}" \
                    --distribution-id "$cf_distribution_id" \
                    --id "$invalidation_id" || {
                    log_warn "CloudFront invalidation wait timed out (non-fatal)"
                }
            fi
        fi
    fi

    log_info "S3 deployment completed successfully"
    return 0
}

deploy_to_lambda() {
    log_info "Starting Lambda deployment..."

    if [[ -z "$LAMBDA_FUNCTION_NAME" ]]; then
        log_error "LAMBDA_FUNCTION_NAME must be set for Lambda deployment"
        return 1
    fi

    local zip_file="${LAMBDA_ZIP_FILE:-./function.zip}"

    # If zip file doesn't exist, try to create it from source directory
    if [[ ! -f "$zip_file" ]]; then
        local lambda_source_dir="${LAMBDA_SOURCE_DIR:-./src}"
        if [[ -d "$lambda_source_dir" ]]; then
            log_info "Creating deployment package from $lambda_source_dir"
            zip_file="/tmp/${APP_NAME}-lambda-$(date +%s).zip"
            (cd "$lambda_source_dir" && zip -r "$zip_file" . -x '*.git*' '__pycache__/*' '*.pyc') 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE" || {
                log_error "Failed to create Lambda deployment package"
                return 1
            }
            TEMP_CRED_FILE="$zip_file"  # Mark for cleanup
        else
            log_error "Neither zip file ($zip_file) nor source directory ($lambda_source_dir) found"
            return 1
        fi
    fi

    log_info "Updating Lambda function: $LAMBDA_FUNCTION_NAME"
    local update_result
    update_result=$(aws lambda update-function-code \
        "${AWS_CLI_ARGS[@]}" \
        --function-name "$LAMBDA_FUNCTION_NAME" \
        --zip-file "fileb://$zip_file" \
        --output json 2>&1) || {
        log_error "Lambda update failed: $update_result"
        return 1
    }

    local function_arn last_modified
    function_arn=$(echo "$update_result" | jq -r '.FunctionArn')
    last_modified=$(echo "$update_result" | jq -r '.LastModified')
    log_info "Lambda updated: $function_arn (Last Modified: $last_modified)"

    # Wait for function to be active
    log_info "Waiting for Lambda function to become active..."
    aws lambda wait function-active-v2 \
        "${AWS_CLI_ARGS[@]}" \
        --function-name "$LAMBDA_FUNCTION_NAME" || {
        log_warn "Lambda function active wait timed out"
    }

    # Publish a new version if requested
    if [[ "${LAMBDA_PUBLISH_VERSION:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
        log_info "Publishing new Lambda version..."
        local version
        version=$(aws lambda publish-version \
            "${AWS_CLI_ARGS[@]}" \
            --function-name "$LAMBDA_FUNCTION_NAME" \
            --description "Deployed at $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" \
            --query 'Version' \
            --output text) || {
            log_warn "Failed to publish Lambda version (non-fatal)"
        }

        if [[ -n "${version:-}" ]]; then
            log_info "Published Lambda version: $version"

            # Update alias if configured
            local alias_name="${LAMBDA_ALIAS:-}"
            if [[ -n "$alias_name" ]]; then
                log_info "Updating Lambda alias '$alias_name' to version $version"
                aws lambda update-alias \
                    "${AWS_CLI_ARGS[@]}" \
                    --function-name "$LAMBDA_FUNCTION_NAME" \
                    --name "$alias_name" \
                    --function-version "$version" 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE" || {
                    log_warn "Failed to update Lambda alias (non-fatal)"
                }
            fi
        fi
    fi

    log_info "Lambda deployment completed successfully"
    return 0
}

# ---- Health Check ----

run_health_check() {
    local health_check_url="${HEALTH_CHECK_URL:-}"

    if [[ -z "$health_check_url" ]]; then
        log_info "No HEALTH_CHECK_URL configured, skipping health check"
        return 0
    fi

    log_info "Running health check: $health_check_url"

    local max_retries="${HEALTH_CHECK_RETRIES:-5}"
    local retry_interval="${HEALTH_CHECK_INTERVAL:-10}"
    local attempt=1

    while [[ $attempt -le $max_retries ]]; do
        local http_code
        http_code=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
            --max-time 10 \
            "$health_check_url" 2>/dev/null) || true

        if [[ "$http_code" == "200" ]]; then
            log_info "Health check passed (HTTP $http_code)"
            return 0
        fi

        log_warn "Health check attempt $attempt/$max_retries failed (HTTP $http_code)"
        ((attempt++))

        if [[ $attempt -le $max_retries ]]; then
            sleep "$retry_interval"
        fi
    done

    log_error "Health check failed after $max_retries attempts"
    return 1
}

# ---- Usage ----

usage() {
    cat <<EOF
Usage: $(basename "$0") [OPTIONS]

AWS Deployment Script - Deploy applications to AWS services.

Options:
    -t, --type TYPE         Deployment type: ecs, s3, lambda (default: $DEPLOYMENT_TYPE)
    -e, --env ENV           Deployment environment (default: $DEPLOYMENT_ENV)
    -r, --region REGION     AWS region (default: $AWS_REGION)
    -p, --profile PROFILE   AWS CLI profile name
    -n, --app-name NAME     Application name (default: $APP_NAME)
    --dry-run               Show what would be done without executing
    -h, --help              Show this help message

Environment Variables:
    AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID       AWS access key (loaded from env/secrets manager)
    AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY   AWS secret key (loaded from env/secrets manager)
    AWS_SESSION_TOKEN       AWS session token (optional)
    AWS_REGION              AWS region
    AWS_PROFILE             AWS CLI profile
    SECRET_MANAGER          Secret source: env, aws-secrets-manager, aws-ssm
    DEPLOYMENT_TYPE         Deployment type: ecs, s3, lambda
    DEPLOYMENT_ENV          Target environment

    ECS Deployment:
        ECS_CLUSTER         ECS cluster name
        ECS_SERVICE         ECS service name
        ECR_REPO            ECR repository name
        DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG    Docker image tag (default: latest)

    S3 Deployment:
        S3_BUCKET           S3 bucket name
        S3_SOURCE_DIR       Local source directory (default: ./build)
        S3_PREFIX           S3 key prefix
        CLOUDFRONT_DISTRIBUTION_ID  CloudFront distribution to invalidate

    Lambda Deployment:
        LAMBDA_FUNCTION_NAME    Lambda function name
        LAMBDA_ZIP_FILE         Path to deployment zip
        LAMBDA_SOURCE_DIR       Source directory (default: ./src)
        LAMBDA_PUBLISH_VERSION  Publish new version (true/false)
        LAMBDA_ALIAS            Alias to update

    Health Check:
        HEALTH_CHECK_URL        URL to check after deployment
        HEALTH_CHECK_RETRIES    Number of retries (default: 5)
        HEALTH_CHECK_INTERVAL   Seconds between retries (default: 10)

Examples:
    # Deploy to ECS using environment credentials
    ECS_CLUSTER=my-cluster ECS_SERVICE=my-service ECR_REPO=my-repo \\
        $(basename "$0") --type ecs --env production

    # Deploy static site to S3
    S3_BUCKET=my-website-bucket \\
        $(basename "$0") --type s3 --env staging

    # Deploy Lambda function
    LAMBDA_FUNCTION_NAME=my-function \\
        $(basename "$0") --type lambda
EOF
}

# ---- Main ----

main() {
    local dry_run=false

    # Parse command-line arguments
    while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
        case "$1" in
            -t|--type)
                DEPLOYMENT_TYPE="$2"
                shift 2
                ;;
            -e|--env)
                DEPLOYMENT_ENV="$2"
                shift 2
                ;;
            -r|--region)
                AWS_REGION="$2"
                shift 2
                ;;
            -p|--profile)
                AWS_PROFILE="$2"
                shift 2
                ;;
            -n|--app-name)
                APP_NAME="$2"
                shift 2
                ;;
            --dry-run)
                dry_run=true
                shift
                ;;
            -h|--help)
                usage
                exit 0
                ;;
            *)
                log_error "Unknown option: $1"
                usage
                exit 1
                ;;
        esac
    done

    log_info "============================================="
    log_info "AWS Deployment Script"
    log_info "============================================="
    log_info "App:         $APP_NAME"
    log_info "Environment: $DEPLOYMENT_ENV"
    log_info "Type:        $DEPLOYMENT_TYPE"
    log_info "Region:      $AWS_REGION"
    log_info "Log file:    $LOG_FILE"
    log_info "============================================="

    if [[ "$dry_run" == "true" ]]; then
        log_info "DRY RUN MODE - No changes will be made"
        check_prerequisites
        authenticate
        log_info "Dry run completed. Would deploy type: $DEPLOYMENT_TYPE"
        exit 0
    fi

    # Step 1: Check prerequisites
    check_prerequisites || exit 1

    # Step 2: Authenticate
    authenticate || exit 1

    # Step 3: Deploy
    case "$DEPLOYMENT_TYPE" in
        ecs)
            deploy_to_ecs || exit 1
            ;;
        s3)
            deploy_to_s3 || exit 1
            ;;
        lambda)
            deploy_to_lambda || exit 1
            ;;
        *)
            log_error "Unknown deployment type: $DEPLOYMENT_TYPE"
            usage
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac

    # Step 4: Health check
    run_health_check || {
        log_error "Post-deployment health check failed"
        exit 1
    }

    log_info "============================================="
    log_info "Deployment completed successfully!"
    log_info "============================================="
}

main "$@"
